title: Memory Persistence
subtitle: Memory Persistence
description: How to store and retrieve user preferences, historical context, and factual knowledge across sessions. The Agent's "long-term memory" system that persists information beyond individual conversations.
sub_problems: Memory storage: selecting appropriate storage backends (vector databases, KV stores, file systems)
Memory retrieval: efficiently retrieving relevant memories
Memory updates: how new information updates or overwrites old memories
Memory expiration: cleanup strategies for outdated information
Cross-session sharing: sharing memories across different sessions
Summary structuring: how to decompose code implementation summaries into queryable structured sections
File progress tracking: accurately tracking implemented/unimplemented file lists after memory cleanup
Capacity-triggered dumping: automatically migrating short-term memory to long-term storage when exceeding thresholds
Experience structuring: how to structure Agent req→resp pairs along with metrics and trajectory data
Dual retrieval backend switching: seamlessly switching between BM25 and vector retrieval for the same experience pool
LLM summary compression: maintaining controllable length for long conversation histories through sliding window segmented summarization
Loop detection: identifying when Agent gets stuck in repetitive output cycles based on memory content
Multimodal two-stage completion detection: expressing state when text processing completes but multimodal processing hasn't
Storage backend delegation: zero-configuration reuse of host framework's storage abstraction layer
Granular embedding: splitting individual memories into multiple semantically independent vector documents for improved matching accuracy
Incremental backfill: efficiently syncing data differences between SQLite and vector databases
Migration version conflicts: ensuring idempotency when multiple migration systems share a version table
best_practices: LLM extraction beats manual logging: LLM captures implicit preferences and context from conversations; manual add_fact is too coarse-grained
Debouncing is mandatory: 30s window + thread_id deduplication prevents LLM call waste in high-frequency conversation scenarios
Confidence gating: not all information deserves storage; 0.7 threshold filters low-value facts
Configuration over hardcoding: all 8 parameters should be configurable, supporting runtime dynamic adjustment
Atomic writes protect data: temp + rename pattern prevents data corruption from interrupted writes
Middleware pattern keeps business logic clean: memory reads/writes shouldn't invade business code
Separate next steps from persistence: LLM-generated next_steps stay in-memory only, avoiding stale suggestions polluting the knowledge base
Event-driven beats round-driven: trigger memory cleanup on write_file calls rather than fixed rounds, precisely matching actual needs
Fuzzy path matching for file tracking: support partial path matching and boundary checks, tolerating LLM-generated path inconsistencies
Watch filtering for writes: only persist Action messages that the Role cares about, avoiding noise pollution in vector databases
Triple-condition gating: check k!=0, user message, and capacity exceeded before retrieving long-term memory, reducing unnecessary vector queries
Read/write separation switches: enable_read/enable_write independently control experience pool behavior, supporting gradual rollout and read-only modes
Dirty marking optimization: is_dirty flag avoids redundant Redis serialization writes when nothing changes
Singleton LLM shares token counting: multiple Agents using the same config_name LLM instance enables unified global cost tracking
Message count truncation is simple but risky: system messages may be dropped; production should add first-message protection
Dual validation with mtime+config_hash: both file modification and parsing config changes auto-invalidate cache, more reliable than mtime alone
atexit fallback + asyncio.gather concurrent finalize: data persists even if users forget manual cleanup
Delegation-based storage beats self-built: reuse host framework's KV storage abstraction; storage backend auto-switches with configuration, zero extra maintenance
3-layer search workflow: index-first then details, 10x token savings, forces Agent to follow search→timeline→get pattern
Granular vector documents: embed narrative/facts/text separately, avoiding semantic dilution from long documents
Idempotent migration checks actual state: PRAGMA table_info detects column existence rather than relying solely on version numbers
MCP protocol replaces direct dependencies: connect to chroma-mcp via stdio MCP, eliminating heavy dependencies like ONNX/WASM

values.DeepCode: Two-layer: ConciseMemoryAgent (workflow summaries) + MemoryStore (daily notes + MEMORY.md)
values.MetaGPT: Four-layer separation: Memory (short-term list) → LongTermMemory (FAISS deduplication) → RoleZeroLTM (Chroma dump) → BrainMemory (Redis + LLM summary)
values.OpenManus: Pydantic Memory model, List[Message] + max_messages sliding window
values.RAG-Anything: Four-layer separation: parse_cache / doc_status / KG+VDB / llm_response_cache
values.claude-mem: Three-layer structure: observations (discoveries) + session_summaries (summaries) + user_prompts (user input), each layer in separate table

values.DeerFlow(102.03): MemoryUpdateQueue 30s debounce + thread_id deduplication + batch processing
values.DeerFlow(1002.05): Gateway REST API runtime dynamic modification
values.DeepCode: Event-driven: write_file tool invocation triggers cleanup + LLM summary generation
values.MetaGPT: Capacity-triggered dump (memory_k=200 threshold) + watch Action filtering for writes
values.OpenManus: Check length after add_message, truncate tail when exceeding limit
values.RAG-Anything: Incremental append to chunks_list + upsert overwrite
values.claude-mem: Hook-driven real-time writes + ChromaSync syncs vector embeddings, incremental backfill for missing records

values.DeerFlow(102.03): LLM structured output (5 fact types + 0.7 confidence threshold)
values.MetaGPT: No independent fact extraction, relies on Message.content holistic storage
values.OpenManus: No fact extraction, stores raw messages only
values.RAG-Anything: LLM-driven entity-relation extraction + multimodal processor description generation
values.claude-mem: XML parser extracts <observation> blocks from SDK responses, ModeManager validates type validity

values.DeerFlow(102.03): Single JSON file + atomic writes (temp + rename)
values.DeepCode: Markdown append (implement_code_summary.md) + date-sliced MD + JSONL sessions
values.MetaGPT: FAISS vector index (disk JSON) + Chroma vector store + Redis KV cache
values.OpenManus: Pure in-memory List, no persistence, lost on process exit
values.RAG-Anything: Delegates to LightRAG KV storage abstraction, supports JSON/PostgreSQL backends
values.claude-mem: SQLite WAL primary + ChromaDB vector store secondary, FTS5 full-text index as third search channel

values.DeerFlow(102.03): MemoryMiddleware after_agent auto-enqueue + format_memory_for_injection() injects <memory> tags
values.DeepCode: Post-cleanup inject initial_plan + file list + knowledge base; read_code_mem MCP tool queries on-demand
values.MetaGPT: find_news vector deduplication filtering + get merges short-term and long-term retrieval results
values.OpenManus: self.messages passed directly to LLM ask_tool, system_msgs concatenated separately
values.RAG-Anything: parse_document cache-first, skip parsing if hit, return directly
values.claude-mem: MCP tools expose search/timeline/get_observations, Agent actively calls to retrieve memories

values.DeepCode: File tracking deduplication: all_files_list vs implemented_files fuzzy path matching
values.MetaGPT: No explicit loop detection, relies on find_news semantic deduplication to indirectly avoid
values.OpenManus: is_stuck() based on assistant message content repetition, threshold 2 occurrences
values.RAG-Anything: doc_status 6-state machine + multimodal_processed boolean flag prevents reprocessing
values.claude-mem: SHA256 content_hash + 30s window deduplication prevents high-frequency duplicate storage

values.DeepCode: get_memory_statistics provides compression ratio and file progress metrics
values.OpenManus: LLM singleton accumulates total_input/completion_tokens + max_input_tokens quota
values.RAG-Anything: parse_cache avoids reparse, llm_response_cache avoids duplicate LLM calls
values.claude-mem: discovery_tokens column records discovery token cost per observation/summary

values.DeepCode: Summary generation supports Anthropic/OpenAI/Google three LLM client routing
values.MetaGPT: BrainMemory distinguishes MetaGPTLLM vs OpenAI two summary paths
values.RAG-Anything: Not applicable (single LightRAG storage abstraction, backend switchable)

values.MetaGPT: Redis TTL 30-minute auto-expiry + is_dirty dirty flag avoids redundant writes
values.RAG-Anything: mtime + config_hash dual validation, file modification or config change auto-invalidates
values.claude-mem: 90-day sliding window filters search results, expired records excluded from semantic ranking

values.MetaGPT: recover_memory startup recovery + clean/clear explicit cleanup + persist manual persistence
values.RAG-Anything: asyncio.gather concurrent finalize + atexit fallback cleanup
values.claude-mem: v23 idempotent migration system, each migration checks actual state not just version number

values.MetaGPT: Message as single vector document, no sub-semantic splitting
values.claude-mem: Each observation's narrative/facts/text split into independent vector documents, improves semantic matching accuracy

values.MetaGPT: ExperiencePool independent from memory system, BM25/Chroma dual backend + LLM Reranker + req→resp structured experience pairs

values.claude-mem: Communicate with chroma-mcp subprocess via stdio MCP, eliminates ONNX/WASM dependencies

solutions.title: Three-layer memory structure with MemoryUpdateQueue debouncing and automatic LLM fact extraction
solutions.description: Three-layer memory stored in memory.json: user layer contains workContext (2-3 sentences)/personalContext (1-2 sentences)/topOfMind (3-5 sentences); history layer contains recentMonths (4-6 sentences)/earlierContext (3-5 sentences)/longTermBackground (2-4 sentences); facts layer is discrete fact list (5 types: preference/knowledge/context/behavior/goal). MemoryUpdateQueue implements 30s debounce (threading.Timer) + thread_id deduplication + threading.Lock concurrency safety + _processing flag prevents re-entrance + 0.5s interval between batch processing. MemoryUpdater uses LLM to analyze conversations and extract structured JSON (6-dimensional context shouldUpdate judgment + newFacts confidence filtering + factsToRemove cleanup), atomic writes (temp + rename). MemoryMiddleware.after_agent() filters messages (keeps only human + AI without tool_calls) before enqueueing.
design_philosophy: LLM-as-Memory-Extractor: LLM understands which information deserves storage better than rules, captures implicit preferences
Debounce-then-Batch: 30s debounce window collects multiple conversation turns before batch processing, balances real-time needs with API costs
Confidence-Gated Fact Storage: 0.7 confidence threshold gates storage, exceeding capacity triggers sorting by confidence with natural forgetting of low-score facts
Middleware-Driven Injection: MemoryMiddleware auto-enqueues at after_agent hook, completely transparent to business code
migration_scenarios: Agent projects needing to remember user preferences across sessions
High-frequency conversation scenarios requiring debouncing to reduce LLM call costs
Projects requiring automatic LLM-driven structured fact extraction instead of manual add_fact

solutions.title: MemoryConfig Pydantic configuration with Gateway API exposure
solutions.description: MemoryConfig (BaseModel) defines 8 configurable parameters: enabled (bool)/storage_path (str)/debounce_seconds (int, ge=1, le=300)/model_name (Optional[str])/max_facts (int, ge=10, le=500)/fact_confidence_threshold (float, ge=0.0, le=1.0)/injection_enabled (bool)/max_injection_tokens (int, ge=100, le=10000). Global singleton pattern: get_memory_config() thread-safe retrieval, load_memory_config_from_dict() loads from dictionary. Gateway API exposes 3 REST endpoints (GET/PUT /api/memory/config + POST /api/memory/config/reset). Consumed by 6 modules: queue/updater/middleware/prompt/gateway router/lead_agent.
design_philosophy: Configuration as documentation: Pydantic BaseModel provides built-in type validation and boundary constraints, self-describing config items
Global singleton + runtime mutability: get_memory_config() ensures global consistency, Gateway API supports runtime dynamic modification
6-module unified config source: queue/updater/middleware/prompt/gateway/lead_agent all read from same config
REST API exposure: frontend can directly call GET/PUT to manage memory configuration without service restart
migration_scenarios: Agent projects needing runtime memory parameter adjustment
Multi-module scenarios requiring shared configuration (avoiding hardcoded defaults across modules)
Projects exposing configuration to frontend management UI via API

solutions.title: Dual-layer memory with session persistence
solutions.description: DeepCode uses write_file event-driven ConciseMemoryAgent to clean conversation history, LLM generates 6-section structured summary appended to implement_code_summary.md, paired with MemoryStore date-sliced and JSONL session persistence.
design_philosophy: Event-driven cleanup: triggered by write_file tool calls rather than fixed intervals
Structured summarization: 6-section format (core_purpose/public_interface/dependencies/notes/next_steps) enables precise retrieval
Persistent session tracking: JSONL format supports incremental append and efficient querying
migration_scenarios: Code generation projects requiring persistent implementation tracking
Multi-turn coding sessions needing structured memory of implementation decisions

solutions.title: Multi-domain memory chain persistence
solutions.description: Three independent memory domains: InvestigateMemory (investigation knowledge chain), SolveMemory (solution step chain), CitationMemory (citation index), each serialized to separate JSON files in output_dir.
design_philosophy: Domain separation: independent memory chains for different reasoning phases
Structured persistence: JSON serialization enables replay and analysis
migration_scenarios: Educational AI systems with distinct investigation and solution phases
Projects requiring citation tracking alongside reasoning chains

solutions.title: Research report caching strategy
solutions.description: Two-layer caching mechanism for research report generation.
design_philosophy: Cache efficiency: reduces redundant research operations
migration_scenarios: Research automation projects with repeated query patterns

solutions.title: Four-layer pluggable storage abstraction
solutions.description: Four abstraction base classes: StorageNameSpace → BaseKVStorage / BaseVectorStorage / BaseGraphStorage / DocStatusStorage, each layer defines standard interface contracts.
design_philosophy: Backend agnostic: storage implementation swappable without code changes
Layered abstraction: separates concerns across KV/vector/graph/document status
Zero-configuration reuse: host framework storage auto-switches with configuration
migration_scenarios: Projects requiring flexible storage backend switching
Multi-backend deployments (JSON for dev, PostgreSQL for production)

solutions.title: Three-layer memory with vector experience pool
solutions.description: Four-layer memory (Memory/LongTermMemory/RoleZeroLTM/BrainMemory) + independent ExperiencePool implements hierarchical persistence: FAISS vector deduplication + Redis LLM summarization + Chroma capacity dumping.
design_philosophy: Hierarchical persistence: short-term → long-term → vector → summarized layers
Semantic deduplication: FAISS prevents duplicate experiences
Capacity-triggered migration: automatic promotion to long-term when exceeding thresholds
migration_scenarios: Multi-agent systems requiring shared experience pools
Projects needing semantic deduplication across large memory volumes

solutions.title: SQLite hybrid search memory system
solutions.description: SQLite + sqlite-vec dual engine: Node.js built-in node:sqlite for relational storage, sqlite-vec extension for vector indexing, FTS5 for full-text search, all three share single SQLite database file.
design_philosophy: Unified storage: single database file simplifies deployment
Multi-index search: vector + full-text + relational queries on same data
Native Node.js: no external dependencies beyond sqlite-vec
migration_scenarios: Node.js projects requiring lightweight hybrid search
Deployments avoiding external vector database services

solutions.title: Sliding window message memory strategy
solutions.description: Pydantic Memory + max_messages sliding window manages conversation history, LLM singleton caches by config_name and accumulates token consumption tracking, is_stuck() detects repetitive output loops.
design_philosophy: Bounded memory: sliding window prevents unbounded growth
Singleton LLM: shared instance enables unified cost tracking
Loop detection: identifies stuck patterns via message repetition
migration_scenarios: Long-running Agent sessions requiring bounded memory
Multi-agent deployments needing unified token accounting

solutions.title: Four-layer KV persistence with document state tracking
solutions.description: RAG-Anything uses LightRAG KV storage to build four-layer persistence (parse_cache/doc_status/KG+VDB/llm_cache), implements cache invalidation via mtime+config_hash dual validation, 6-state machine tracks text+multimodal two-stage processing progress.
design_philosophy: Multi-layer caching: parse/status/knowledge/response caches reduce redundant work
Dual validation: mtime+config_hash catches both file and configuration changes
State machine: 6-state model precisely tracks multimodal processing stages
migration_scenarios: RAG systems processing mixed text and multimodal content
Projects requiring precise document processing state tracking

solutions.title: ChromaDB dual-layer memory persistence
solutions.description: SQLite WAL + ChromaDB vector store dual architecture enables cross-session memory: granular embedding (narrative/facts as independent vector documents) + 3-layer search workflow (search→timeline→get) achieves 10x token savings.
design_philosophy: Dual persistence: SQLite for structured data, ChromaDB for semantic search
Granular embedding: split observations into independent vectors for precise matching
3-layer search: index-first approach reduces token consumption
MCP integration: stdio protocol eliminates heavy dependencies
migration_scenarios: Long-running conversational AI requiring semantic memory search
Projects prioritizing token efficiency in memory retrieval
Deployments avoiding external vector database services
